titleEn: Elbit Systems acquires Bluewhite: a new chapter for autonomous farming
titleZh: 埃尔比特系统收购Bluewhite：自主农业新篇章
summaryZh: 以色列国防与科技集团埃尔比特系统通过其子公司FUSE完成了对农业自动驾驶技术公司Bluewhite的收购。Bluewhite专注于果园、葡萄园等永久性作物的精准作业，已积累数十万小时实际运行经验，并与凯斯纽荷兰（CNH）旗下New Holland品牌建立多阶段合作。此次收购将加速智能自主系统在农业中的规模化部署，结合物联网与云计算实现实时决策与预测分析，反映农业自动化从试验走向主流的大趋势。
featuredReason: 该收购案例展示了军工级技术向农业自动化领域的跨界赋能，对智慧畜牧行业探索高精度、高可靠性自主系统在养殖环境（如精准饲喂、巡检机器人）的应用具有重要借鉴意义。
contentFull: Elbit Systems acquires Bluewhite: a new chapter for autonomous farming 02-06 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintBluewhite focused from the outset on permanent crops such as fruit trees and grapevines – a segment where precision and consistency are critical. Photo: BluewhiteIsraeli defence and technology group Elbit Systems has completed the acquisition of Blue White Robotics, better known as Bluewhite, through its subsidiary FUSE. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Tel Aviv, the company has established itself as one of the most promising players in autonomous vehicle technology for agriculture.With the acquisition by Elbit Systems’ FUSE, Bluewhite gains access to considerably greater resources, technological expertise and a global network. Bluewhite CEO Ben Alfi described the deal as an exciting new chapter. He believes the combination of Bluewhite’s proven autonomy expertise and Elbit Systems’ advanced technologies and extensive operational experience will accelerate the deployment of intelligent autonomous systems in some of the world’s most demanding environments.For the agricultural sector, this means the technology Bluewhite has developed can be further refined and rolled out at greater scale. Integration with IoT devices and cloud computing will expand its capabilities further still — enabling real-time decision-making, predictive analytics and an adaptability that helps farmers navigate the growing unpredictability of climate and markets.The acquisition of Bluewhite by Elbit Systems reflects a broader shift taking place across the agricultural sector. Autonomous machines are no longer experimental. With a proven platform, hundreds of thousands of operational hours and now the backing of one of Israel’s largest technology companies, Bluewhite is well positioned to expand its role further. Bluewhite has a multi-phase partnership with New Holland, a brand of CNH, to collaborate on distribution, manufacturing and integration of Bluewhite’s autonomous solutions for New Holland tractors in North America. Photo: New HollandReal-world experienceWhat sets Bluewhite apart from many other technology companies in the agricultural sector is its depth of real-world experience. The company has now accumulated hundreds of thousands of cumulative autonomous operating hours across agricultural and defence applications. It is a proven system deployed daily under real conditions, in orchards, vineyards and arable fields.Bluewhite focused from the outset on permanent crops such as fruit trees and grapevines – a segment where precision and consistency are critical. Autonomous machines navigate narrow row spacings, carry out tasks such as spraying and crop monitoring, and do so with a level of accuracy that human operators struggle to maintain over long working days. 2 years ago, Bluewhite announced a multi-phase partnership with New Holland, a brand of CNH, to collaborate on distribution, manufacturing and integration of Bluewhite’s autonomous solutions for New Holland tractors in North America. This partnership enables New Holland tractors to operate fully autonomously in orchards, vineyards and other specialty crop operations.Practical autonomyBluewhite was founded with a clear mission: to bring practical autonomy to environments where there are no paved roads. Agriculture proved to be the ideal starting point. Farmers worldwide are grappling with a growing shortage of labour, rising costs and mounting pressure to produce more sustainably. Bluewhite saw an opportunity and developed a platform that converts existing farm vehicles into fully autonomous machines – without requiring farmers to replace their trusted tractors.The platform centres on two products. Pathfinder is an autonomous retrofit kit that transforms conventional vehicles into self-driving machines. The system is OEM-agnostic, meaning it works regardless of the brand or type of vehicle. Compass is the accompanying cloud-based software that allows farmers to manage, monitor and control their autonomous fleet remotely. Together, they form a complete ecosystem for autonomous farming.Join 17,000+ subscribersSubscribe to our newsletter to stay updated about all the need-to-know content in the agricultural sector, two times a week. translationZh:
以色列国防与科技集团埃尔比特系统（Elbit Systems）已通过其子公司FUSE完成了对Blue White Robotics（更广为人知的名称为Bluewhite）的收购。该公司成立于2017年，总部位于特拉维夫，已确立自身作为农业自动驾驶技术领域最具前景的企业之一。

通过埃尔比特系统旗下FUSE的收购，Bluewhite获得了显著更多的资源、技术专长和全球网络。Bluewhite首席执行官Ben Alfi将这笔交易描述为一个激动人心的新篇章。他认为，Bluewhite经过验证的自主技术专长与埃尔比特系统的先进技术和广泛运营经验相结合，将加速智能自主系统在全球一些要求最苛刻的环境中的部署。

对于农业领域而言，这意味着Bluewhite开发的技术可以进一步优化并以更大规模推广。与物联网设备和云计算的集成将进一步扩展其能力——实现实时决策、预测分析以及适应性，帮助农民应对日益不可预测的气候和市场。

埃尔比特系统收购Bluewhite反映了农业领域正在发生的更广泛转变。自主机器已不再是试验性产品。凭借经过验证的平台、数十万小时的运行时长，以及如今以色列最大科技公司之一的背书，Bluewhite已做好充分准备，进一步扩大其角色。

**实际经验**

Bluewhite与农业领域许多其他科技公司的不同之处在于其深厚的实际经验。该公司目前在农业和国防应用中累计了数十万小时的自主运行时长。这是一个经过验证的系统，每天在果园、葡萄园和大田作物中实际部署运行。

Bluewhite从一开始就专注于果树和葡萄藤等永久性作物——这是一个对精准度和一致性要求极高的细分领域。自主机器能够在狭窄的行间距中导航，执行喷洒和作物监测等任务，并且以人类操作员在长时间工作中难以维持的精度水平完成这些工作。两年前，Bluewhite宣布与凯斯纽荷兰（CNH）旗下品牌New Holland建立多阶段合作，在北美地区就Bluewhite自主解决方案的分销、制造和集成展开协作。
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Israeli defence and technology group Elbit Systems has completed the acquisition of Blue White Robotics, better known as Bluewhite, through its subsidiary FUSE. Founded in 2017 and headquartered in Tel Aviv, the company has established itself as one of the most promising players in autonomous vehicle technology for agriculture.
